rulings.Case Title: RONALD KEELING VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Pneumo Abex, LLC¿s motion to compel further responses to written discovery: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re: Deposition Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Pacific Gas & Electric Company¿s motion for protective order is denied. PG&E did not demonstrate good cause why it should not have to produce the requested documents or why the deposition of its person most knowledgeable and/or custodian of records should not go forward, as required by C.C.P. 2025.420. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs¿ Motion To Compel Pacific Gas Electric Company To Produce Its Person(S) Most Knowledgeable, Custodian(S) Of Records, And Responsive Documents At Deposition Pursuant To Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel Pacific Gas & Electric Company to produce its person(s) most knowledgeable, custodian(s) of records and responsive documents at deposition pursuant to subpoena is granted. PG&E did not demonstrate that it has already produced the documents at issue. PG&E has the burden of finding a deponent to produce. If, after a reasonable and diligent search and education, PG&E determines that no such person exists, PG&E may so state on the record at the deposition. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiff Interrogatories To Defendants From Fisher Controls International Llc; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories to defendants from Fisher Controls International LLC, for an order staying defendant from filing, or having heard, any dispositive motions in asbestos cases before this court until 60 days after defendant has provided verified responses, and for sanctions of $1161 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
